title: Warehouse handler
description:
Rockwell Automation is a global technology leader focused on helping the world’s manufacturers be more productive, sustainable, and agile. With more than 28,000 employees who make the world better every day, we know we have something special. Behind our customers - amazing companies that help feed the world, provide life-saving medicine on a global scale, and focus on clean water and green mobility - our people are energized problem solvers that take pride in how the work we do changes the world for the better.

We welcome all makers, forward thinkers, and problem solvers who are looking for a place to do their best work. And if that’s you we would love to have you join us!

Job Description

As a Warehouse Handler, you will ensure smooth operations for new supplier parts receipt, production hold parts, and inventory accuracy.

You will work with the warehouse team and report to the Warehouse Team Lead and work onsite in Kancheepuram, India.

Your Responsibilities:

Manage new supplier parts receipt and tracking in SAP and WMS systems.

Segregate MRB parts and scrap materials from production and suppliers as per quality guidelines.

Maintain accurate MRB and scrap inventory records in SAP and WMS systems.

Perform inventory cycle counts for MRB and scrap stock and execute SAP adjustments on time.

Post scrap transactions in SAP and ensure compliance with disposal processes.

Coordinate scrap disposal with approved vendors, ensuring proper documentation (Gate Pass, Disposal Certificate).

Prepare and maintain scrap and MRB reports using MS Excel and share with Quality and EHS teams.

Coordinate with receiving an kitting team also will do the supporting role of these functions.

Ensure safety and environmental compliance during scrap handling and disposal.

The Essentials - You Will Have:

Diploma / Degree in logistics, supply chain management, or a related field is preferred.

Minimum of 3 years experience in similar field

Strong knowledge of warehouse management applications and Microsoft Office suite.

Familiarity with warehouse management systems (WMS) and proficiency in using relevant software applications.

Strong computer skills, including proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, and Outlook).

Ability to operate warehouse equipment, such as forklifts or pallet jacks, and obtain necessary certifications if required.

Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

Ability to work flexible hours, including evenings and weekends, as required by operational needs.

The Preferred - You Might Also Have:

3+ years of experience in automotive warehouse

Hands-on experience in SAP transactions (GRN, TO, cycle count, scrap posting).

Familiarity with Lean practices and inventory accuracy improvement projects.

What We Offer:

Our benefits package includes …

Comprehensive mindfulness programs with a premium membership to Calm

Company volunteer and donation matching program – Your volunteer hours or personal cash donations to an eligible charity can be matched with a charitable donation.

Employee Assistance Program

Personalized wellbeing programs through our OnTrack program

On-demand digital course library for professional development

... and other local benefits!

At Rockwell Automation we are dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive and authentic workplace, so if you're excited about this role but your experience doesn't align perfectly with every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right person for this or other roles.

#LI-Onsite, #LI-NB1

title: Product Manager – ERP Finance & AI Automation
description:
Rockwell Automation is a global technology leader focused on helping the world’s manufacturers be more productive, sustainable, and agile. With more than 28,000 employees who make the world better every day, we know we have something special. Behind our customers - amazing companies that help feed the world, provide life-saving medicine on a global scale, and focus on clean water and green mobility - our people are energized problem solvers that take pride in how the work we do changes the world for the better.

We welcome all makers, forward thinkers, and problem solvers who are looking for a place to do their best work. And if that’s you we would love to have you join us!

Job Description

Summary:

The Product Manager – AI & ERP Automation will lead the definition, delivery, and evolution of AI‑powered ERP automation use cases, with an initial focus on Plex Finance. This role will own product strategy and execution for finance automation initiatives while also contributing to AI opportunities across broader ERP domains such as supply chain and procurement. The role is based in Pune (Balewadi) and follows a hybrid work model (3 days in office: Monday, Tuesday, Thursday). This is a long‑term growth role with strong career progression opportunities as Plex's AI portfolio expands.

You will report to Group Product Manager, Work location Pune.

Your Responsibilities:
Own end‑to‑end product management for AI‑driven ERP Finance automation, starting with:
Accounts Payable (AP) automation
Bank reconciliation automation
Journal entry automation
Cash‑flow prediction and finance intelligence
Translate business problems into clear product requirements, epic stories, and prioritized roadmaps
Partner closely with engineering, data science, UX, and architecture teams to deliver scalable AI solutions
Drive solution design, development execution, and delivery readiness for committed releases
Collaborate with stakeholders across Finance, Supply Chain, and ERP domains to identify and shape future AI automation opportunities (procurement, requisitioning, shipping, receiving, etc.)
Balance short‑term delivery commitments with long‑term product vision
Ensure solutions are aligned to customer value, measurable outcomes, and business growth goals
Actively contribute to Plex's AI strategy and product positioning within Rockwell Automation
The Essentials - You Will Have:
5+ years of experience in Product Management, preferably within ERP, Finance Systems, or Enterprise Software
Demonstrated exposure to AI/ML‑driven products or automation use cases
Solid understanding of ERP Finance processes (AP, GL, reconciliation, cash flow, controls)
Ability to work effectively with cross‑functional, global teams
Strong communication skills with a collaborative, ownership‑driven mindset

The Preferred - You Might Also Have:
5+ years of experience with ERP platforms, finance automation, or enterprise SaaS products
Familiarity with AI concepts such as workflow automation, predictive analytics, intelligent document processing
Experience influencing roadmap decisions in complex enterprise environments
What We Offer:

Our benefits package includes …

Comprehensive mindfulness programs with a premium membership to Calm
Volunteer Paid Time off available after 6 months of employment for eligible employees
Company volunteer and donation matching program – Your volunteer hours or personal cash donations to an eligible charity can be matched with a charitable donation.
Employee Assistance Program
Personalized wellbeing programs through our OnTrack program
On-demand digital course library for professional development

... and other local benefits!

At Rockwell Automation we are dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive and authentic workplace, so if you're excited about this role but your experience doesn't align perfectly with every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right person for this or other roles.

title: Project Engineer - Hardware
description:
Rockwell Automation is a global technology leader focused on helping the world’s manufacturers be more productive, sustainable, and agile. With more than 28,000 employees who make the world better every day, we know we have something special. Behind our customers - amazing companies that help feed the world, provide life-saving medicine on a global scale, and focus on clean water and green mobility - our people are energized problem solvers that take pride in how the work we do changes the world for the better.

We welcome all makers, forward thinkers, and problem solvers who are looking for a place to do their best work. And if that’s you we would love to have you join us!

Job Description

Pune/Noida, India

As a Project Engineer – Hardware, you will be responsible for delivering high-quality hardware design solutions for industrial automation projects. You will work closely with the Team Lead / Lead Engineer to execute project deliverables, ensuring compliance with technical specifications, engineering standards, and customer requirements. Your role will span the complete project lifecycle—from system design to commissioning—while driving cost efficiency, standardization, and customer satisfaction. You will report to the Manager – Execution / Team Lead.

Your Responsibilities:

Hardware Design & Engineering: Design and develop control systems, including panel layouts, control circuits, MCC systems, and enclosure designs etc.. Define system specifications, I/O architecture, and ensure hardware-software compatibility.

Project Execution & Delivery: Execute assigned work packages and projects on time with high quality and cost effectiveness, ensuring alignment with customer expectations and technical requirements.

Technical Analysis & Problem Solving: Analyze existing systems, identify design gaps or deficiencies, and implement corrective actions to ensure robust system performance.

Standards & Compliance: Ensure all deliverables adhere to engineering standards (IEC/RA) and promote maximum reuse of standard products and engineering practices.

System Integration & Coordination: Coordinate with cross-functional teams for subsystem design and ensure seamless integration into the overall system architecture.

Risk & Issue Management: Collaborate with Team Leaders to identify, mitigate, and resolve technical risks and issues during project execution.

Continuous Improvement & Cost Optimization: Drive engineering efficiency through process improvements, reusable designs, and optimization of hardware solutions.

Customer Focus & Acceptance: Work closely with internal stakeholders and customers to ensure successful project implementation and achieve customer acceptance.

The Essentials - You Will Have:

Bachelor's degree in Electrical / Instrumentation / Electronics / Control Engineering
2–5 years of experience in industrial automation hardware design
Proficiency in AutoCAD (preferably AutoCAD Electrical) or ePlan
Working knowledge of DOL, Star-Delta starters, VFDs, Soft Starters, PLCs, and MCC system design
Experience in design calculations (heat load, cable sizing, etc.)
Strong understanding of enclosure design (GA/Layout)
Knowledge of IEC and relevant electrical design standards
Experience across full project lifecycle (design to commissioning)
Good communication skills (written and verbal)

The Preferred - You Might Also Have:

Experience with Rockwell Automation (ControlLogix, CompactLogix, PLC-5) or Siemens/Schneider platforms
Expertise in Integrated Architecture, drives, motor control, and safety systems
Knowledge of industrial networks and control system architectures
Ability to develop control circuits based on process requirements
Exposure to Lean Six Sigma, basic project management, and EHS practices
Strong application and industry domain knowledge
Capability to mentor junior engineers and share best practices

What We Offer:

Our benefits package includes …

Comprehensive mindfulness programs with a premium membership to Calm
Volunteer Paid Time off available after 6 months of employment for eligible employees
Company volunteer and donation matching program – Your volunteer hours or personal cash donations to an eligible charity can be matched with a charitable donation.
Employee Assistance Program
Personalized wellbeing programs through our OnTrack program
On-demand digital course library for professional development

... and other local benefits!

title: Proposal Administrator
description:
Rockwell Automation is a global technology leader focused on helping the world’s manufacturers be more productive, sustainable, and agile. With more than 28,000 employees who make the world better every day, we know we have something special. Behind our customers - amazing companies that help feed the world, provide life-saving medicine on a global scale, and focus on clean water and green mobility - our people are energized problem solvers that take pride in how the work we do changes the world for the better.

We welcome all makers, forward thinkers, and problem solvers who are looking for a place to do their best work. And if that’s you we would love to have you join us!

Job Description

Proposal Administrator

Pune-India

As a proposal administrator you will have to manage the operational proposal, RFQ, Customer PO orders, Co-ordinate with order team, Finance, Tax and Billing team, etc.

You may need to contact distributor/Customer/ Regional stakeholders for prompt action.

Your Responsibilities:

Execute processes in compliance with established SOPs and guidelines. Performs a wide variety of administrative/Operational    duties for assigned team(s) & function(s) globally. Responsible for gathering and summarizing enabling data to execute assigned tasks. Responsible for providing administrative support on activities related to end-to-end proposal activities Work with required regional & domestic stakeholders to ensure compliance & smooth processing of the activities/processes assigned. Demonstrates accountability for completing work assignments given in the form of process objectives & goals; meets commitments and deadlines. Maintains the confidentiality of sensitive and proprietary technical, financial and commercial information. Present availability on parts if relevant / when requested, working in collaboration with Planning Team through business system interface. Maintain the project/contract/proposals/quotation status in Business systems throughout its lifecycle including creation, material / service supply requests, receipts, invoicing, completion and closure. Extract data (from business systems, SAP / IFS / others) & publish necessary reports required by the various regional stakeholders as per the established cadence & guidelines. Contribute to Root cause analysis for any deviation highlighted by the regional stakeholders, required documentation of the said deviations and responding to audit processes as & when required. Use the various official tools available like conference calls & emails for frequent interaction with peers/ customers & regional stakeholders on discussion related to processes, contracts, feedback, presentations & other updates on a regular basis. Coordinate with multiple teams across organization for required updates, timely material delivery/availability, receipts, invoicing, completion and closure of project / contracts.

The Essentials - You Will Have:

Bachelor’s degree in business administration or related field. 
Hands-on experience with SAP 
2+Years of experience in business operations 
Strong Skills in Microsoft excel for working with data, Solving Problem and drawing insight from different sources 
Understanding of Presales/Proposals  
A team Player who works well with different departments  
Skilled in handling multiple tasks and shifting priorities 

The Preferred - You Might Also Have:

Experience working with advance excel, words,etc.
Familiarity with Power automate and power apps for process automation and application developments

Other Experiences  Preferred too: P2P , Quote to Cash, Supply Chain, relevant Administrator experiences.

What We Offer:

Our benefits package includes …

Comprehensive mindfulness programs with a premium membership to Calm
Volunteer Paid Time off available after 6 months of employment for eligible employees ((REMOVE BULLET FOR MANUFACTURING ROLES))
Company volunteer and donation matching program – Your volunteer hours or personal cash donations to an eligible charity can be matched with a charitable donation. 
Employee Assistance Program 
Personalized wellbeing programs through our OnTrack program 
On-demand digital course library for professional development

... and other local benefits!

title: ISC Data Analyst
description:
Rockwell Automation is a global technology leader focused on helping the world’s manufacturers be more productive, sustainable, and agile. With more than 28,000 employees who make the world better every day, we know we have something special. Behind our customers - amazing companies that help feed the world, provide life-saving medicine on a global scale, and focus on clean water and green mobility - our people are energized problem solvers that take pride in how the work we do changes the world for the better.

We welcome all makers, forward thinkers, and problem solvers who are looking for a place to do their best work. And if that’s you we would love to have you join us!

Job Description

Summary:

We are excited to invite you to join our dynamic ISC Analytics and Reporting Team, where you will play a key role in shaping our data strategy. As a member of this global team, you will report to the ISC Analytics and Reporting Manager and collaborate closely with IT and various functional stakeholders to develop and maintain Power Platform solutions, Power BI reports, dataflows, and other data-related assets. You will have the opportunity to work with talented colleagues from around the world, exchanging ideas and contributing to innovative solutions that drive value for our stakeholders. We value diverse perspectives and encourage applicants from all backgrounds.

You will be reporting to SR. MANAGER, DATA ANALYTICS and have a hybrid schedule working in Bangalore, India.

Your Responsibilities:
In this role, you will need a strong passion for data and the ability to provide solutions that benefit our stakeholders.
The Data Analyst must understand how business applications support processes, how information transforms throughout these processes, and the information architecture required to support analytics across supply chain functional domains.
This role offers opportunities to expand your skills and grow your career, while working in a supportive environment that encourages ongoing learning and professional development.
We are committed to supporting your success and providing resources for continuous growth.

The Essentials - You Will Have:
Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Business, or Supply Chain preferred; other bachelor's-level degrees will be considered.
Flexibility on shift timing to overlap with global teams.
Minimum of 3 years of experience with MS Azure or similar data infrastructure, and database knowledge.
3+ years demonstrated experience with Power BI (or equivalent).
Knowledge of AI tools such as agents and copilot (or equivalent), PowerApps, Power Automate, Databricks, Python, and SQL.

The Preferred - You Might Also Have:
Microsoft PL300 (Power BI Data Analyst Associate) Certification.
Data storytelling experience.
SAP ecosystem knowledge.
Ability to adapt to changing business requirements.
Experience building end-to-end data solutions/models with a focus on integration.
Direct firsthand experience designing and developing data solutions.
Ability to decompose an idea into a process, identify the supporting data, and define supporting data structures.
Domain experience supporting one of the following functional areas: Purchasing / Sourcing; Materials (supply / demand planning / inventory management); Logistics (transportation / warehousing); Global Trade Management (import/export, classification); Quality.

What We Offer:

title: Third Party Product Security Engineer
description:
Rockwell Automation is a global technology leader focused on helping the world’s manufacturers be more productive, sustainable, and agile. With more than 28,000 employees who make the world better every day, we know we have something special. Behind our customers - amazing companies that help feed the world, provide life-saving medicine on a global scale, and focus on clean water and green mobility - our people are energized problem solvers that take pride in how the work we do changes the world for the better.

We welcome all makers, forward thinkers, and problem solvers who are looking for a place to do their best work. And if that’s you we would love to have you join us!

Job Description

Summary:

As a Third-Party Product Security Engineer, you will play a critical role in Third party risk & compliance (TPRC) program to strengthen supply chain security, product compliance, and secure qualification of third-party components.

You will lead supplier security assessments with closed-loop remediation, support secure product qualification, and drive supplier improvements across the ecosystem.

This role requires close collaboration with Business Units, Product Security, and global suppliers to embed secure development practices and operationalize regulatory requirements (e.g ..EU CRA, MR, NIS2), while driving continuous improvement.

Strong stakeholder engagement and influencing skills are critical to building partnerships and advancing supply chain security outcomes.

Your Responsibilities:

Lead evaluation, risk-based qualification, and onboarding of suppliers and partners, ensuring understanding of TPRC frameworks and product security requirements
Conduct supplier security assessments with closed-loop remediation tracking, improving and compliance with Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL) and regulatory requirements (e.g., EU CRA, NIS2)
Review supplier design controls and secure software development practices (verification and validation, risk management, configuration management, build/release governance) to ensure understanding of industry and product security standards
Partner with Business Units, Product Security, and cross-functional teams. Together, perform Threat Analysis & Risk Assessments (TARA), design-for-security reviews, and secure qualification of third-party software and firmware components.
Collaborate with other teams and suppliers to guide enterprise-wide adoption of supply chain security requirements
Work with Sourcing and Legal teams to support Quality and Security Agreements, and strengthen supplier governance and purchasing controls
Lead investigation of security and quality issues, ensuring root cause analysis (RCA), CAPA execution, risk mitigation, and closure
Promote understanding of strategic suppliers on product security standards, processes, and compliance expectations
Ensure readiness transition of suppliers from development to production across security, quality, and compliance requirements
Support post-market product security activities, including software anomalies, complaint handling, RCFA, and CAPA closure
Deliver training, workshops, and enablement sessions to other teams and suppliers to guide adoption of security and compliance practices
Maintain accountability for execution, ensuring you manage and resolve risks improving product security.
Influence and collaborate across a global, matrixed organisation to lead risk reduction and secure product outcomes
Ensure end-to-end understanding across the Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL) and supplier lifecycle
Present risk, quality, and compliance insights to leadership, including supplier performance, assessment outcomes, and remediation progress

The Essentials – You Will Have:

Bachelor's degree in Electrical/Electronics Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field
5+ years of experience in Product Security, Cybersecurity, Software Engineering, Software QA, or Systems Engineering
5+ years of experience conducting supplier security assessments, audits, and SDLC/SDL evaluations
Hands-on experience with Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL) and frameworks such as NIST SSDF (800-218), IEC 62443-4-1/4-2, or equivalent
Experience in risk-based assessments, remediation tracking, and programme governance
Experience supporting regulatory compliance (e.g., EU CRA, NIS2, ISO 27001)
Experience influencing and driving outcomes across a global, matrixed environment

The Preferred – You Might Also Have:

Certification in Lean / Six Sigma (Green Belt / Black Belt)
Experience with Agile methodologies (Scrum, SAFe, Lean)
Experience in Third-Party Risk Management (TPRM) and supplier/OEM ecosystems
Familiarity with software/firmware product qualification and security/compliance tools (e.g., Jira, OneTrust, GRC platforms)
Exposure to regulatory frameworks such as EU CRA, NIS2, or similar global compliance standards

What We Offer:

Our benefits package includes …

Comprehensive mindfulness programmes with a premium membership to Calm
Volunteer Paid Time off available after 6 months of employment for eligible employees
Company volunteer and donation matching programme – Your volunteer hours or personal cash donations to an eligible charity can be matched with a charitable donation.
Employee Assistance Program
Personalised wellbeing programmes through our OnTrack programme
On-demand digital course library for professional development

... and other local benefits!

At Rockwell Automation, we are dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive, and authentic workplace. If you're excited about this role but your experience doesn't align perfectly with every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right person for this or other roles.

title: Major Incident Manager
description:
Rockwell Automation is a global technology leader focused on helping the world’s manufacturers be more productive, sustainable, and agile. With more than 28,000 employees who make the world better every day, we know we have something special. Behind our customers - amazing companies that help feed the world, provide life-saving medicine on a global scale, and focus on clean water and green mobility - our people are energized problem solvers that take pride in how the work we do changes the world for the better.

We welcome all makers, forward thinkers, and problem solvers who are looking for a place to do their best work. And if that’s you we would love to have you join us!

Job Description

Major Incident Manager

Pune-India

Rockwell seeks a proactive IT professional to lead a technical support team in resolving major IT incidents. Key duties include managing incident bridge calls, coordinating resources, ensuring service level agreements, and communicating with IT leadership and customers. The role also focuses on improving incident and root-cause analysis processes, collaborating with investigation and problem management teams to implement corrective actions. This position reports to the Team Lead (Reporting Manager) and follows a hybrid schedule in Pune , India.

You will Report to the Team Lead.

Your Responsibilities:

Lead and manage bridge calls to expedite incident resolution.

Provide regular status updates to IT leadership and customers regarding current IT issues and actions taken.

Work to reduce Mean Time to Detect (MTTD) and Mean Time To Resolve (MTTR), while increasing Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF).

Facilitate daily operations meetings to review major incidents, emergencies and scheduled changes, and activities influencing IT services.

Identify and implement opportunities to optimize incident and problem management for performance and cost efficiency.

Collaborate with IT Service Management (ITSM) teams to coordinate responses to Priority 1 and 2 incidents (P1/P2) and perform administrative tasks across various technological platforms.

Maintain disciplined ticketing practices for Incident, Change, and Problem Management.

Escalate major P1, P2, and P3 incidents as needed and coordinate with the CISO on security operations.

Partner with management to implement initiatives that drive process effectiveness and improve customer experience.

Participate in an on-call rotation with other Incident Managers to provide coverage during off-hours, weekends, or employee absences.

Perform additional duties assigned in support of IT Service Management and the Rockwell Virtual Integrated Command Center (VICC).

The Essentials - You Will Have:

ITIL Foundations V3 or V4 Certification.

Proven leadership abilities in managing cross-functional tasks, including working with vendors and managed service providers.

At least 3-5 years'equivalent experience managing IT incidents, changes, or problems within a global organization.

Proficient in analytical tools related to Incident, Change, and Problem Management.

Solid understanding of IT Operations and core systems.

Excellent verbal and written English communication skills.

The Preferred - You Might Also Have:

Bachelor's Degree or equivalent; minimum of 5 years and maximum 7 years' relevant experience in IT incident management.

Ability to approach incidents from diverse perspectives and collaborate with teams to determine appropriate interim and permanent solutions.

Working knowledge of networking, Windows and UNIX operating systems, servers, databases, application development, and identity access management.

What We Offer:

Our benefits package includes …

Comprehensive mindfulness programs with a premium membership to Calm

Volunteer Paid Time off available after 6 months of employment for eligible employees.

Company volunteer and donation matching program – Your volunteer hours or personal cash donations to an eligible charity can be matched with a charitable donation.

title: Salesforce Engineer
description:
Rockwell Automation is a global technology leader focused on helping the world’s manufacturers be more productive, sustainable, and agile. With more than 28,000 employees who make the world better every day, we know we have something special. Behind our customers - amazing companies that help feed the world, provide life-saving medicine on a global scale, and focus on clean water and green mobility - our people are energized problem solvers that take pride in how the work we do changes the world for the better.

We welcome all makers, forward thinkers, and problem solvers who are looking for a place to do their best work. And if that’s you we would love to have you join us!

Job Description

Salesforce Engineer

Pune, Bangalore, Hyderabad, Noida, India

As a Salesforce Engineer, you will design, develop, and deploy innovative Salesforce solutions that support Rockwell Automation’s customer engagement and digital transformation initiatives. You will report to the Technical Leader and work in a hybrid capacity from our Pune, India office. You will collaborate with architects, analysts, and business stakeholders to build scalable, secure, and high-performing applications across Salesforce Cloud platforms.

Your Responsibilities:

Develop custom Salesforce solutions using Apex, Visualforce, Lightning Components, and JavaScript frameworks.
Have knowledge oof Salesforce Experience Cloud Module.
Integrate Salesforce with other enterprise systems using REST/SOAP APIs and middleware tools like MuleSoft.
Participate in solution design with architects and business analysts, ensuring alignment with business goals and technical standards.
Perform unit testing, system integration testing, and support UAT to ensure solution quality.
Contribute to release planning, deployment, and documentation using tools like GitHub, Gearset, and OwnBackup.

The Essentials – You Will Have:

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, MIS, Engineering, or a related field.
Minimum 3 years of experience as a Salesforce developer.
Proficiency in Apex, Visualforce, Lightning Components, and JavaScript.
Strong understanding of Salesforce architecture, data model, and security model.
Experience with version control systems and CI/CD practices.

The Preferred – You Might Also Have:

Salesforce certifications (e.g., Platform App Builder, Developer I & II).
Experience with CTI integration, email automation, and knowledge management.
Familiarity with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript frameworks (e.g., Angular, React).
Experience with Salesforce Experience Cloud.

Ensure safety and environmental compliance during scrap handling and disposal.

Diploma / Degree in logistics, supply chain management, or a related field is preferred.
Minimum of 3 years experience in similar field
Strong knowledge of warehouse management applications and Microsoft Office suite.
Familiarity with warehouse management systems (WMS) and proficiency in using relevant software applications.
Strong computer skills, including proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, and Outlook).
Ability to operate warehouse equipment, such as forklifts or pallet jacks, and obtain necessary certifications if required.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Ability to work flexible hours, including evenings and weekends, as required by operational needs.
The Preferred - You Might Also Have:
3+ years of experience in automotive warehouse
Hands-on experience in SAP transactions (GRN, TO, cycle count, scrap posting).
Familiarity with Lean practices and inventory accuracy improvement projects.

title: AP Demand Planner
description:
Rockwell Automation is a global technology leader focused on helping the world’s manufacturers be more productive, sustainable, and agile. With more than 28,000 employees who make the world better every day, we know we have something special. Behind our customers - amazing companies that help feed the world, provide life-saving medicine on a global scale, and focus on clean water and green mobility - our people are energized problem solvers that take pride in how the work we do changes the world for the better.

We welcome all makers, forward thinkers, and problem solvers who are looking for a place to do their best work. And if that’s you we would love to have you join us!

Job Description

As a Demand Planning Specialist, you will be responsible for managing short-, mid-, and long-term demand forecasting (0–24 months) for standard products across the APAC region. You will work closely with cross-functional teams including supply chain, manufacturing, finance, and global planning to ensure optimal inventory levels and service performance. This role operates in a collaborative, fast-paced environment and typically follows a hybrid work model.

You will report to the Manager, AP Demand Planning and will be working from Noida, India.

Your Responsibilities

Develop and manage monthly short-, mid-, and long-term demand forecasts (0–24 months) for standard products.

Support execution of the short-term demand planning process, including risk evaluation related to inventory and customer service levels.

Integrate internal, external, quantitative, and qualitative inputs to improve forecast accuracy.

Identify supply gaps using key metrics and collaborate with global planning teams to implement mitigation actions.

Conduct regular business and performance reviews with stakeholders.

Analyze forecast accuracy of metrics, identify root causes, and implement corrective actions.

Participate in product lifecycle management, including NPI and phase-outs.

Partner with global teams to achieve inventory targets and optimize supply-demand balance.

Manage projects in ambiguous situations using data-driven decision-making.

The Essentials – You Will Have

Bachelor’s degree in Logistics, Business, Engineering, or a related field.

Minimum 3+ years of experience in supply chain, preferably in demand planning.

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, Power BI, and ERP systems (APO preferred).

Fluency in English (written and verbal).

The Preferred – You Might Also Have

APICS certification (CPIM or CSCP).

Experience in global or matrix organizations.

Exposure to advanced demand planning tools and analytics.

Experience working with distributors and suppliers.

What We Offer

Our benefits package includes …

Comprehensive mindfulness programs with premium membership to Calm.

Volunteer Paid Time off available after 6 months of employment for eligible employees.

Company volunteer and donation matching program – Your volunteer hours or personal cash donations to an eligible charity can be matched with a charitable donation.

Employee Assistance Program

Personalized wellbeing programs through our OnTrack program.

On-demand digital course library for professional development

... and other local benefits!

title: IBE Analyst
description:
Rockwell Automation is a global technology leader focused on helping the world’s manufacturers be more productive, sustainable, and agile. With more than 28,000 employees who make the world better every day, we know we have something special. Behind our customers - amazing companies that help feed the world, provide life-saving medicine on a global scale, and focus on clean water and green mobility - our people are energized problem solvers that take pride in how the work we do changes the world for the better.

We welcome all makers, forward thinkers, and problem solvers who are looking for a place to do their best work. And if that’s you we would love to have you join us!

Job Description

Principal duties and responsibilities:

Execute processes in compliance with established SOPs and guidelines. Performs a wide variety of administrative/Operational    duties for assigned team(s) & function(s) globally. Responsible for gathering and summarizing enabling data to execute assigned tasks. Responsible for providing administrative support on activities related to a customer order; from receipt through closure including procurement of goods & services from specified suppliers that are moderate to high in complexity under minimal supervision and in accordance with all relevant policies & procedures Work with required regional & domestic stakeholders to ensure compliance & smooth processing of the activities/processes assigned. Demonstrates accountability for completing work assignments given in the form of process objectives & goals; meets commitments and deadlines. Maintains the confidentiality of sensitive and proprietary technical, financial, and commercial information. Present availability on parts if relevant / when requested, working in collaboration with Planning Team through business system interface. Maintain the project/contract/proposals/quotation status in Business systems throughout its lifecycle including creation, material / service supply requests, receipts, invoicing, completion and closure. Extract data (from business systems, SAP / IFS / others) & publish necessary reports required by the various regional stakeholders as per the established cadence & guidelines. Contribute in Root cause analysis for any deviation highlighted by the regional stakeholders, required documentation of the said deviations and responding to audit processes as & when required. Use the various official tools available like conference calls & emails for frequent interaction with peers/ customers & regional stakeholders on discussion related to processes, contracts, feedback, presentations & other updates on a regular basis. Coordinate with multiple teams across organization for required updates, timely material delivery/availability, receipts, invoicing, completion and closure of project / contracts.

Leadership

Act as a Point of Contact for acknowledging & addressing internal customers queries related to tasks assigned Participate & actively contribute to continuous improvement initiatives & reporting/documenting enhancements to improve productivity. Enthusiastic to work & adapt in a dynamic, fast paced and challenging environment. Able to manage difficult situations in a professional and commercial manner Self-motivated, flexible and someone who holds self-accountable for completing own tasks/responsibilities, but also contributes to others ’Efforts’, as required Work closely with stakeholders for the optimization of processes, identify process improvement opportunities and areas for continuous improvement.

Interpersonal

Ability to effectively interface with internal and external stakeholders (in varying roles and departments); provide succinct information on time & as requested. Strong passion for delivering excellent customer experience. Excellent communicator at all levels (Written & Verbal) with strong ability to clearly articulate & convey the understanding to peers & customers.

Education & Experience:

Bachelor’s Degree in Science/ Commerce/ Business Administration or equivalent. Minimum of 3-5 years of relevant experience in Business Operations. Should have exposure to Global Work style, engagement with clients while sitting at remote locations. Willing to work in flexible business shifts / global time zones including NA/LA time zone to drive business stakeholder connect. Prior experience of working in business operations with hands-on experience of Data analytics & reporting, MS Excel, MS Power point, handling large data sets, attention to details, complex problem solving, Data management & query management, SAP Contracts / Order entry as required would be preferred. Prior knowledge on commercial aspects of Contracts/Projects are a plus Exposure around audits and services contracts is a plus Lean Six Sigma certifications are a plus.

title: QA Engineer
description:
Rockwell Automation is a global technology leader focused on helping the world’s manufacturers be more productive, sustainable, and agile. With more than 28,000 employees who make the world better every day, we know we have something special. Behind our customers - amazing companies that help feed the world, provide life-saving medicine on a global scale, and focus on clean water and green mobility - our people are energized problem solvers that take pride in how the work we do changes the world for the better.

We welcome all makers, forward thinkers, and problem solvers who are looking for a place to do their best work. And if that’s you we would love to have you join us!

Job Description


Job Description

Rockwell Automation is a global technology leader focused on helping the world's manufacturers be more productive, sustainable. With more than 28,000 employees who make the world better every day, we know we have something special. Behind our customers - amazing companies that help feed the world, provide life-saving medicine on a global scale, and focus on clean water and green mobility - our people are energized problem solvers that take pride in how the work we do changes the world for the better.

We welcome all makers, forward thinkers who are looking for a place to make an impact. And if that's you we would love to have you join us!

As a Quality Engineer, you will play a crucial role in ensuring the quality and reliability of our software products. With a minimum of 3 years of experience, you will leverage your expertise to meticulously test applications, identify and document defects, and collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver high-quality software solutions. Your attention to detail and problem-solving skills will be key in maintaining the integrity and performance of our applications.


Key Responsibilities: 

Participate proactively in standup meetings, sprint planning, story grooming sessions, team retrospectives, and implementing improvements.
Interacting with business teams on initiatives and how they should be approached from a testing standpoint.
Develop detailed, comprehensive, and well-structured test plans and test cases based on project requirements and specifications.
Ensure coverage of functional, regression, integration, and user acceptance testing.
Develop test cases, scenarios, and scripts based on user stories, business requirements, and technical specifications.
Perform manual testing to identify defects, ensure application functionality, and validate that requirements are met.
Execute tests in different environments and across various platforms to ensure compatibility and responsiveness.
Identify, log, and track software defects.
Provide detailed descriptions, steps to reproduce, and severity assessments for identified issues.
Work closely with developers, product managers, and other stakeholders to understand project requirements and communicate testing progress and results.
Generate and present test reports to stakeholders, highlighting key findings and recommendations for improvement.

Basic Qualifications:

Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent relevant experience
3+ years’ experience with manual testing
Strong English speaking and writing skills.

Preferred Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Engineering, or similar technical discipline
Typically requires a minimum of 3 years of related experience.
Experience in systems development lifecycle.
Experience with Agile development methodologies.
Hand-on experience in manual testing Web-Based, Desktop-Based and SAP Applications.
Intellectual curiosity and the ability to question thought partners across functional areas.
Ability to adapt quickly to new technologies and changing business requirements.
Solid problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and critical thinking abilities.
Excellent understanding of business complexity and project interdependencies.
Ability to partner with multiple stakeholders and influence project decisions.
Strong teamwork, customer service and organizational skills.
Certification in automation testing tools like Tosca are preferred. Any other certification is a plus.
Familiar with Data Integrity testing including experience with database technologies, query building (DAX, MDX, SQL), ETL concepts, cloud-based data solutions, and business intelligence solutions.
Good to have knowledge in Tosca Data Integrity and qTest.

What We Offer:

title: Senior GST Analyst
description:
Rockwell Automation is a global technology leader focused on helping the world’s manufacturers be more productive, sustainable, and agile. With more than 28,000 employees who make the world better every day, we know we have something special. Behind our customers - amazing companies that help feed the world, provide life-saving medicine on a global scale, and focus on clean water and green mobility - our people are energized problem solvers that take pride in how the work we do changes the world for the better.

We welcome all makers, forward thinkers, and problem solvers who are looking for a place to do their best work. And if that’s you we would love to have you join us!

Job Description

As a Senior GST Analyst, you will be part of the Tax & Finance function and will be responsible for managing end-to-end indirect tax compliance, advisory, and process optimization. You will work closely with cross-functional teams.

You will be reporting to the Manager, Tax and working onsite in Delhi, India. 

Your Responsibilities: 

Indirect tax compliance

Monthly tax computation, Timely payment and filing of returns for GST

Manage changes to be made in SAP processes to implement the changes made in tax regulations

Work closely with cross functions like Finance, logistics, Customer care to ensure availability of required information/documents

Tax Risk assessment, mitigation and optimization

Provide/ arrange documents and prepare working for tax assessment

Provide/ arrange documents and prepare working for refund claim applications

Provide/ arrange documents and prepare working for Stat. Audit, GST audit, Internal Audit etc.

Indirect tax litigation and manage relationship. 

Arrange/collects information and documents as required to justify the position taken by organization on disputed issue

Co-ordinate with consultants/counsels to assess the merits of the issue involved as disputed by tax authority

Held meetings with various cross functions to address their concerns and provide solution to issues in time line manner

Indirect Tax Advisory

Advise on day-to-day basis the business units and cross functions on indirect tax matters in time line manner

Tax Accounting 

Preparation & submission of monthly reconciliation to Global Finance Operation team

Timely and precise reconciliation of all the indirect tax accounts

Timely posting of monthly entries related to GST

Tax Automation and Process Improvement

Understand existing process of preparation of GSTR-3B, GSTR-1, RCM working thoroughly and then lead the project to automate the process and apply best standard practices

Review all tax positions in the GST return are in line with GST laws

Develop mechanism and process to prepare various reconciliations of sales, GST liability, GST-ITC (Books VS GST Portal/ 3B VS 2A/2B) in the best possible manner

Identify improvement areas in the tax function and then take required necessary steps

GST Presentations & Trainings

Prepare GST dashboards on monthly basis to present high level summary of status of compliance, impediments etc.

Provides trainings to entire finance team (ITC, RCM etc.)

Regular tax updates on news alerts, circulars, notifications and potential impact on the Company

Prepare GST related PPT’s and SOP (standard procedures of working) followed during working

The Essentials – You Will Have: 

Qualified Chartered Accountant. 

5+ years of post-qualification experience in indirect taxation with large organizations or firms. 

Expert-level understanding of GST laws, rules, and compliance requirements

Hands-on experience working on ERP/SAP-based integrated systems. 

What We Offer:

Comprehensive mindfulness programs with a premium membership to Calm

description:

With more than 28,000 employees who make the world better every day, we know we have something special. Job Description

Rockwell Automation is a global technology leader focused on helping the world's manufacturers be more productive, sustainable. With more than 28,000 employees who make the world better every day, we know we have something special. Behind our customers - amazing companies that help feed the world, provide life-saving medicine on a global scale, and focus on clean water and green mobility - our people are energized problem solvers that take pride in how the work we do changes the world for the better.

We welcome all makers, forward thinkers who are looking for a place to make an impact. And if that's you we would love to have you join us!

Job Description

The RA SAP ABAP functional developer is responsible for designing, coding, and modifying SAP applications to meet the Global Revenue Management and Rockwell Automation specific requirements, in adherence to the RA IT Methodologies. They work closely with the GRM team and other RA SAP functional personnel to implement and deploy changes to SAP and other integrated or interfaced systems, such as Vistex and PROS.

In addition, they are expected to manage the construction of code based on a technical design, following RA IT development standards, while work with project teams and technical resources, ensure all code is well documented and can be navigated by others in the next phase (testing) within the RA IT Agile frame.

This role will report to the Team Lead - IT.

Key Responsibilities:

Develop and maintain SAP design and technical specification documents, and conduct unit, integration and release testing to validate functionality
Support end-users Acceptance Testing activities, and participate in Production Operations and Release Management support;
Assist with upgrades, new releases and monitor performance of the technical objects;
BAPIs, BADIs, RFCs, IDOCs, LSMW, writing user exits, BDC coding, and interfaces;
SAP ABAP/4 programming using: SAPScript, iDocs, BAPI, RFC; -
Develop and maintain SAP design and technical specification documents; assist in defining and documenting SAP Best Practices in an Agile Framework
Code change and code review;
Analyze and fix the issues related to Gateway OData; - Preferred, not required
Develop enhancements;
Analyze and Fix IDOC, Web Service, RFC Issues;
Writing technical specifications.
3 – 5 years of SAP ABAP development in an enterprise environment is ideal
Proactive mindset;
The ability to work alone
SAP UI5 and Fiori-L3 preferred
SAP ABAP-L3 (Mandatory).

The Essentials - You Will Have:

Bachelor's Degree in Relevant Field.
1 – 4 years of experience in ABAP Programming is required
Experienced in RICEF developments
Strong analytical problem-solving skills
Good communication skills

The Preferred - You Might Also Have:

Programming experience in SD & Vistex modules
Experience in SAP Enhancement Framework
Experience in ABAP Object Oriented Programming
Knowledge in web services (SOAP/REST/HTTP POST)
Knowledge in web developments (PHP, HTML, XSLT, XML, JSON)

description:
Rockwell Automation is a global technology leader focused on helping the world’s manufacturers be more productive, sustainable, and agile. With more than 28,000 employees who make the world better every day, we know we have something special. Behind our customers - amazing companies that help feed the world, provide life-saving medicine on a global scale, and focus on clean water and green mobility - our people are energized problem solvers that take pride in how the work we do changes the world for the better.

We welcome all makers, forward thinkers, and problem solvers who are looking for a place to do their best work. And if that’s you we would love to have you join us!

Job Description

Position Summary:

Rockwell Automation's Safety, Sensing & Industrial Components Business is looking for an experienced Software Team Lead. This lead will lead a product team of engineers on all stages of the software development lifecycle, including design, implementation, and testing activities for industry-leading software. You will be experienced in the areas of engineering management, including recruitment, task assignments, technical mentoring, performance evaluations, people development, etc. The candidate will collaborate with Cross Functional, Software, Architecture, and Test engineering peers to ensure the development of high-quality products.

You will report to the Manager, Software Engineering and have a hybrid schedule working in Pune, India.

Your Responsibilities:
Guide new product support continuation engineering projects with superior quality and on-time delivery
Responsible for continuous development/ improvement and motivating a software development team.
Partner with other teams to drive alignment and collaboration. Act as a bridge between technical teams, project stakeholders, and leadership.
Devise project plans, schedules, and present to relevant stakeholders, drive execution
Responsible for team building, frequent assessment and evaluation of direct reports
Mentor, coach, and inspire team members to achieve their full potential
Foster an empathetic, collaborative, and inclusive team culture
Provide clear guidance, feedback, and support to unblock execution teams
Drive innovation to improve efficiency, quality, and speed of delivery
Support recruitment and developing onboarding/ training plans.

The Essentials - You Will Have:
Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering or equivalent.
A minimum of 2 years' experience in leading a team of direct reports of individual contributors/ engineers.
A minimum of 10+ years of experience in Software development with a proven track record of delivering high-quality software on time.
Experience developing software using Agile Scrum/ SAFe.
Experience in building Team and culture, people development.
Strong multitasking ability with an appetite for switching priorities effectively.
Able to travel locally and internationally if required.

The Preferred - You Might Also Have:
Experience developing products that are Functional Safety certified.
Knowledge of safety standard IEC 61508.
Understanding of communication .protocols, such as RS-232, USB, Ethernet, IO Link, CIP.
Proficient in Angular, C#, .net.
Proven ability to analyze and solve complex problems involving hardware and Software.
Experience with standard version control systems.
Familiarity with Rockwell Automation controllers, and software tools.
collaboration with global teams.
Plugged into emerging technologies including AI and identify opportunities to integrate them into projects.
